2604.10702.title: Architecture-Agnostic Modality-Isolated Gated Fusion for Robust Multi-Modal Prostate MRI Segmentation
2604.10702.paper_summary: Multi-parametric prostate MRI -- combining T2-weighted, apparent diffusion coefficient, and high b-value diffusion-weighted sequences -- is central to non-invasive detection of clinically significant prostate cancer, yet in routine practice individual sequences may be missing or degraded by motion, artifacts, or abbreviated protocols. Existing multi-modal fusion strategies typically assume complete inputs and entangle modality-specific information at early layers, offering limited resilience when one channel is corrupted or absent. We propose Modality-Isolated Gated Fusion (MIGF), an architecture-agnostic module that maintains separate modality-specific encoding streams before a learned gating stage, combined with modality dropout training to enforce compensation behavior under incomplete inputs. We benchmark six bare backbones and assess MIGF-equipped models under seven missing-modality and artifact scenarios on the PI-CAI dataset (1,500 studies, fold-0 split, five random seeds). Among bare backbones, nnUNet provided the strongest balance of performance and stability. MIGF improved ideal-scenario Ranking Score for UNet, nnUNet, and Mamba by 2.8%, 4.6%, and 13.4%, respectively; the best model, MIGFNet-nnUNet (gating + ModDrop, no deep supervision), achieved 0.7304 +/- 0.056. Mechanistic analysis reveals that robustness gains arise from strict modality isolation and dropout-driven compensation rather than adaptive per-sample quality routing: the gate converged to a stable modality prior, and deep supervision was beneficial only for the largest backbone while degrading lighter models. These findings support a simpler design principle for robust multi-modal segmentation: structurally contain corrupted inputs first, then train explicitly for incomplete-input compensation.
2604.10702.paper_summary_zh: 多參數前列腺MRI——結合T2加權、表觀擴散係數和高b值擴散加權序列——對於無創檢測臨床顯著的前列腺癌至關重要，然而在日常實踐中，個別序列可能因運動、伪影或簡化的協議而缺失或劣化。現有的多模態融合策略通常假設完整的輸入，並在早期層中糾纏模態特定的信息，當一個通道受到損壞或缺失時，提供的韌性有限。我們提出了模態隔離門控融合（MIGF），這是一個架構無關的模塊，在學習的門控階段之前保持獨立的模態特定編碼流，並結合模態隨機失活訓練，以強化在不完整輸入下的補償行為。我們基準測試了六個基本骨幹，並在PI-CAI數據集（1,500項研究，fold-0拆分，五個隨機種子）下評估了配備MIGF的模型在七種缺失模態和伪影場景中的表現。在基本骨幹中，nnUNet提供了性能和穩定性的最佳平衡。MIGF將理想場景下的UNet、nnUNet和Mamba的排名分數分別提高了2.8%、4.6%和13.4%；最佳模型MIGFNet-nnUNet（門控 + ModDrop，無深度監督）達到了0.7304 +/- 0.056。機理分析顯示，穩健性增益來自於嚴格的模態隔離和基於隨機失活的補償，而非自適應的每樣本質量路由：門控收斂到穩定的模態先驗，深度監督僅對最大的骨幹有益，而對輕量模型則有損害。這些發現支持了一種更簡單的設計原則，以實現穩健的多模態分割：首先結構性地包含損壞的輸入，然後明確訓練以補償不完整的輸入。
2604.10702.author: Yongbo Shu et.al.
2604.10702.authors: Yongbo Shu, Wenzhao Xie, Shanhu Yao, Zirui Xin, Luo Lei, Kewen Chen, Aijing Luo
